tasks.title: `void this.startDecode(job)` discards the returned promise â€” if startDecode throws outside its own try/catch it becomes an unhandled rejection

tasks.title: manifestDispatched gate never reset on job retry, so onManifest never fires after first error recovery
tasks.defer_reason: The gate correctly fires once per job lifetime, which is the intended contract (onManifest should fire exactly once). The scenario where prefetchManifest sets job.manifest after manifestDispatched=true (from startDecode) is a confirmed race (see contracts-9/concurrency-1), but in that case the manifest was already dispatched, so the consumer already received it. Whether 'never fires after first error recovery' is a real failure depends on whether manifestDispatched gets set before the error â€” cannot determine from code alone.

tasks.title: nextTier returns null for 'full' but callers can set targetTier below currentTier causing permanent silent exclusion
tasks.category: enum not handled by all consumers
tasks.defer_reason: The filter at line 473 (`tierRank(j.currentTier) < tierRank(j.targetTier)`) prevents any job where targetTier <= currentTier from being scheduled. A setTargetTier-style call that lowers targetTier below currentTier would cause permanent silent exclusion. However the public API does not expose setTargetTier â€” cannot confirm without knowing the full API surface.
